description: Canonical cross-binding nRPC compatibility fixture for the client-streaming + duplex shapes (Phase 2 NRPC_BINDINGS_PLAN) plus the observer + metrics-snapshot surface (S1-A1 of NRPC_STREAMING_PARITY_AND_GO_BINDING). ABI v3 is additive over v2: the streaming shapes documented below are unchanged from v2, plus new observer_invariants + metrics_snapshot_invariants sections that pin the cross-binding RpcCallEvent / RpcMetricsSnapshot shapes. Two services, each with a documented contract and a fixed set of ok_cases. Every binding's wire-format compat test loads this file and asserts: encoding the request stream on each side, dispatching through the canonical handler, and decoding the response stream yields outputs that semantically match the fixture. Status codes, header names, and the JSON shapes are all part of the contract.

wire_snapshots.description: Byte-exact hex snapshots of canonical RPC payload encodings, taken at the wire level (after meta serialization). Bindings re-encoding the same logical inputs MUST produce the same bytes; drift on endianness, padding, header packing, or flag-bit layout is an inter-binding wire bug, not a semantic difference the JSON-only ok_cases would catch. The substrate-side test wire_snapshots_match_fixture exercises the Rust codec against these; per-binding ports run the same assertion against their native encoders.

request_grant_payload.endianness_note: call_id is u64 little-endian; credits is u32 big-endian. The asymmetry mirrors encode_stream_grant from Phase 3 — bindings MUST preserve it.

canonical_flag_end_placement.rule: FLAG_RPC_REQUEST_END is carried by the LAST chunk that contains user data, not a trailing empty-body terminator. Bindings emitting `request_items = [a, b, c]` MUST set FLAG_END on the chunk carrying `c` (not on a fourth empty chunk). For the 1-item case the FLAG_END rides on the ONE chunk that carries that item. For the empty-items case, the initial REQUEST itself carries FLAG_RPC_CLIENT_STREAMING_REQUEST | FLAG_RPC_REQUEST_END with an empty body (the 'pure terminator' fast path).
canonical_flag_end_placement.rationale: Trailing-empty-FLAG_END was tolerated by the Rust substrate's terminator-semantics rule (empty body + FLAG_END = pure terminator, suppressed from the handler's stream) but it costs an extra event per call. Pinning the data-bearing-FLAG_END as canonical keeps all bindings byte-identical on the wire and avoids the substrate's 'is_pure_terminator' branch.

examples.zero_items: REQUEST(flags = CLIENT_STREAMING_REQUEST | REQUEST_END, body = []). Zero REQUEST_CHUNKs.
examples.one_item: REQUEST(flags = CLIENT_STREAMING_REQUEST | REQUEST_END, body = encode(item_0)). Zero REQUEST_CHUNKs.
examples.n_items: REQUEST(flags = CLIENT_STREAMING_REQUEST, body = encode(item_0)). REQUEST_CHUNK(flags = 0, body = encode(item_1)) ... REQUEST_CHUNK(flags = REQUEST_END, body = encode(item_{N-1})).

error_cases.description: Cross-binding-pinned error paths. Bindings MUST surface the codes/status documented here for the given trigger; the cross_lang_streaming integration test exercises each via the same direct-fold-dispatch pattern as the ok_cases.

cases.name: client_stream_decode_failure
cases.trigger: Caller emits a REQUEST_CHUNK whose body is not valid JSON for the request_item_shape (e.g., the raw bytes `not-json-shape`).
cases.diagnostic_substring: typed streaming handler: bad request body

cases.name: client_stream_handler_application_error
cases.trigger: Server-side handler returns Err(String) (typed) or raises the typed application exception (Node/Python).

cases.name: client_stream_cancel_mid_send
cases.trigger: Caller drops the call handle after sending at least one chunk and before finish().

cases.name: duplex_cancel_from_caller
cases.trigger: Caller drops BOTH halves (or the combined DuplexCall) before clean close.
cases.diagnostic_substring: server observed CANCEL during duplex handler execution

cases.name: duplex_decode_failure
cases.trigger: Server emits a RESPONSE chunk whose body is not valid JSON for response_shape.

services.description: Drain a stream of {text, numbers} request items. Return a single response: { echo: concatenation of all text fields joined by ' ', total: sum of all numbers across all items, count: number of items consumed }.

response_shape.total: i64 — sum of all numbers across items

services.description: Drain a stream of {text, numbers} request items. Emit one response per request item: { echo: item.text, sum: sum of item.numbers }. After processing all items, emit one final summary response: { echo: 'summary', sum: count_of_items }. Handler returns Ok after EOF on the request stream.

response_shape.echo: str — item's text, or 'summary' for the final frame
response_shape.sum: i64 — item's numbers summed, or count_of_items for the summary

observer_invariants.description: Cross-binding contract for the RpcCallEvent shape surfaced by SetObserver (NRPC_STREAMING_PARITY_AND_GO_BINDING.md S1-A1 / S2-A1 / S2-A2). Every binding's observer dispatcher receives an event with these fields populated, and every typed wrapper layer normalizes the binding-specific POD shape into a tagged-union status discriminator. The fixture documents the cross-binding invariants; per-binding tests pin their own field-name casing (`statusKind` in Node, `status_kind` in Python, `status_kind` in C ABI POD, `Status: RpcCallStatus{...}` in Go).

fields.name: caller
fields.semantic: Local node id (== local_node_id on Outbound events)

fields.name: latency_ms
fields.semantic: Wall-clock ms; truncated from the substrate's Duration::as_millis()

fields.name: request_bytes
fields.semantic: Wire payload size of request body (0 when not available)

fields.name: response_bytes
fields.semantic: Wire payload size of response body (0 when not available)

fields.name: direction
fields.semantic: 'outbound' for caller-side, 'inbound' for server-side (v1 emits only 'outbound')

fields.name: ts_unix_ms
fields.semantic: Unix-ms timestamp at fire time; 0 on pre-1970 clocks

status_discriminator.description: Substrate's `RpcCallStatus` is a tagged union. The C ABI flattens it to (status_kind: u8, status_message: optional-string); the napi POD uses (statusKind: string, statusMessage: optional string); the pyo3 POD uses (status_kind: string, status_message: optional string); the Go typed wrapper reconstructs an interface union. Cross-binding contract: same four variants, same string discriminators where applicable, message present ONLY on the Error variant.

direction_discriminator.description: Substrate's `RpcDirection`. C ABI flattens to u8; higher-level bindings use the string form.

firing_contract.description: Locked decision #1 (NRPC_STREAMING_PARITY_AND_GO_BINDING.md). Observers fire synchronously from the substrate dispatch path. Each binding's docstring spells out 'callbacks must be cheap; push into a queue for slow consumers.' Implementations:
firing_contract.napi: TSFN with ThreadsafeFunctionCallMode::NonBlocking — overflow drops the event.
firing_contract.pyo3: Tokio blocking-pool worker — GIL acquisition off the dispatch thread.
firing_contract.c_abi: Direct C function-pointer call on the substrate dispatch task — no thread hop, Go side MUST be cheap.
firing_contract.v1_scope: Only direction=='outbound' events fire. Inbound (server-side handler invocation) is a planned follow-up.

metrics_snapshot_invariants.description: Cross-binding contract for the RpcMetricsSnapshot shape returned by MetricsSnapshot (S1-A1 / S2-A1 / S2-A2). Every binding emits the same logical fields in the same order; only field-name casing differs per binding convention.

envelope.ordering: Sorted by service name. Empty list iff no service has been called since mesh construction.

service_metrics_fields.name: in_flight
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Currently-in-flight calls (signed to absorb hedge-loser underflow during transient races)

service_metrics_fields.name: latency_sum_ns
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Sum of resolved-call latencies in nanoseconds

service_metrics_fields.name: latency_buckets
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Cumulative histogram bucket counts; index i corresponds to DEFAULT_LATENCY_BUCKETS_SECS[i]; last entry is +Inf bucket and equals latency_count

service_metrics_fields.name: handler_invocations_total
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Total handler invocations on this node

service_metrics_fields.name: handler_panics_total
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Handler panics caught by the fold's catch_unwind

service_metrics_fields.name: handler_duration_sum_ns
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Sum of handler durations in nanoseconds

service_metrics_fields.name: handler_duration_buckets
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Cumulative bucket counts for handler duration

service_metrics_fields.name: streaming_chunks_emitted_total
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Total streaming chunks emitted via RpcResponseSink::send

service_metrics_fields.name: streaming_chunks_dropped_total
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Total streaming chunks dropped by the per-call pump mpsc at sink.send() time (overflow signal)

service_metrics_fields.name: capability_denied_total
service_metrics_fields.semantic: Inbound calls rejected by the capability-auth callee-side gate before handler invocation

per_binding_field_naming.description: Each binding's preferred field-name casing. Bindings re-serializing a snapshot to JSON for downstream consumers SHOULD use the substrate's snake_case form; only the in-process typed surface uses camelCase or PascalCase per language convention.
per_binding_field_naming.go: PascalCase struct fields with `json:"snake_case"` tags (the JSON wire form matches the C ABI's emitted shape)

scrape_cost.description: Snapshot construction is one DashMap iteration on the substrate side — O(n_services) loads, no locks held. Safe to call on every Prometheus scrape.
scrape_cost.go_method: MetricsSnapshot(rpc) — JSON decode of net_rpc_metrics_snapshot output
